mappings.rationale: Directly addresses aesthetic experience, apprehension, and appreciation of works of art — the core of this section's part A.
Covers art criticism, interpretation, and evaluation criteria including aesthetic, formal, and social criteria — central topics of this section.
Addresses scholarship in the arts, critical methods, and the relationship between art scholarship and other humanistic disciplines.
Covers diverse critical approaches and methods for interpreting and evaluating literary works, directly relevant to the section's treatment of criticism and critical methods.
Explores aesthetic criteria of evaluation and the philosophical basis for appreciating works of art, relevant to the section's discussion of aesthetic experience and evaluation.
Directly relevant to the interpretation of works of art, meaning in art, and interpretive methods of criticism — key topics in this section.
Addresses critical approaches to art that examine social, moral, and political significance of artworks — relevant to the section's discussion of evaluation criteria and social context.
Directly relevant to the section's discussion of symbol and myth in the arts as aspects of interpretation and meaning.
Addresses mythological symbols and narratives as they appear across artistic traditions, relevant to the section's coverage of symbol and myth in the arts.
Relevant to the section's discussion of journalistic criticism as a critical style and the role of media in public reception of art.
Directly relevant to the section's coverage of remuneration of artists, protection of their rights, piracy, and plagiarism.
Relevant to the section's treatment of social control of art, censorship, and related forms of regulation.
Relevant to the section's discussion of the arts in education and aesthetic education.
Relevant to the section's discussion of the role of industry and commerce in disseminating art and the economics of the art market.
Addresses postmodern critical approaches to evaluating art, challenging traditional aesthetic criteria — relevant to the section's discussion of diverse criteria of evaluation.
Explores deconstructive and poststructural approaches to interpreting texts and artworks, directly relevant to the section's treatment of critical approaches and methods.
Barthes's semiotic analysis of cultural texts and artworks is directly relevant to the section's treatment of meaning in art, interpretation, and critical methods.
Foucault's work on discourse, power, and cultural institutions is relevant to the section's treatment of social control of art and institutional contexts.
Derrida's deconstructive method is a major critical approach to the arts, relevant to the section's discussion of critical approaches and interpretation.
Adorno's aesthetic theory and cultural criticism address art's social functions, the culture industry, and criteria for evaluating art — directly relevant to multiple parts of this section.
Relevant to the section's discussion of the interaction of arts with social and cultural institutions, and social uses of art.
Addresses the contemporary art market, institutional contexts, and critical reception of art — relevant to the section's economics of art and criticism discussions.
Explores the experience of reading as aesthetic apprehension, interpretation, and appreciation of literary works — relevant to the section's treatment of aesthetic experience.
Relevant to the section's discussion of censorship and social control of art, and the interaction of arts with religion.
Relevant to the section's treatment of censorship, social control of art, and the regulation of artistic expression.
Explores how imagination functions in aesthetic experience and the apprehension of artworks, relevant to the section's treatment of aesthetic experience and interpretation.
Relevant to the section's discussion of the training and work of the artist, and the relation of the creative process to criticism and appreciation.
Addresses cross-cultural and comparative critical methods in literature, relevant to the section's treatment of scholarship and critical approaches in the arts.
